description: Capability registry for lore harness adapters. Each framework profile names the support level (`full`, `partial`, `fallback`, `none`) for every capability lore depends on. Every non-`none` cell points to an evidence id defined in adapters/capabilities-evidence.md so callers can audit when the claim was last verified. Lore code branches on capability support, not framework name (D1, D6, D8 in the multi-framework-agent-support plan).

support_levels.full: Native support that matches lore's protocol semantics with no degradation.
support_levels.partial: Native surface exists but does not cover every semantic lore requires; degraded notes describe the gap.
support_levels.fallback: No native surface; lore implements the capability through a wrapper, MCP shim, or lead-side post-hoc check. Behavior is best-effort and not equivalent to native.
support_levels.none: Capability is unavailable on this harness; callers must degrade explicitly and surface the unsupported state in `lore status`.

capabilities.instructions: Loads framework-agnostic protocol text (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md style) into every session.
capabilities.skills: Discovers and loads SKILL.md files at session start so user-invoked /<skill> commands route to lore protocols.
capabilities.mcp: Registers Model Context Protocol servers with the harness so lore-provided tools are reachable from agents.
capabilities.session_start_hook: Fires a lore-controlled hook at session start; used to load knowledge, work, threads, and pending capture queues.
capabilities.stop_hook: Fires a lore-controlled hook at agent stop / end-of-turn; used to gate session-end on plan persistence.
capabilities.pre_compact_hook: Fires a lore-controlled hook before context compaction; used to prompt thread/work updates before context is dropped.
capabilities.tool_hooks: Fires lore-controlled hooks before/after tool calls; used by guard-work-writes and similar invariant enforcers.
capabilities.permission_hooks: Allows lore-installed policy to approve or deny tool invocations (PreToolUse-style block decisions).
capabilities.task_completed_hook: Fires a lore-controlled hook at worker task completion that can block on a missing/invalid completion report.
capabilities.subagents: Spawns fresh subagent contexts for fanout (worker, researcher, reviewer) so each receives a clean context window.
capabilities.team_messaging: Inter-agent messaging primitive (TeamCreate / SendMessage / shared team-state) so a lead can coordinate workers in flight.
capabilities.transcript_provider: Exposes a parseable session/transcript artifact that capture, digest, novelty, and ceremony-detection scripts can consume.
capabilities.headless_runner: Non-interactive `<harness> -p <prompt>` mode used by audit-artifact.sh (correctness-gate, curator, reverse-auditor) and other batch judges.
capabilities.plugin_runtime: Native plugin/extension runtime where lore can register code (TypeScript, shell, etc.) that participates in lifecycle events.
capabilities.model_routing: Whether the harness exposes a single model/provider per session or can route per-spawn to multiple providers; values: `single` or `multi`. Route resolvers use the closed frameworks keyset to recognize a framework-qualified role value, then validate the remaining native binding against the selected target's shape. The optional `tiers` array lists the harness's validated model aliases in ascending capability order (consumed by the settlement panel's tier cycle and framework_model_routing_tiers); an empty array means no validated alias ladder — consumers must not guess one.
capabilities.interaction: Per-framework probed PTY interaction contract (sibling of `capabilities` and `model_routing`, like `model_routing`). Each row is an evidence-gated cell — `support` from the closed level set plus an `evidence` id resolving in capabilities-evidence.md — carrying one typed payload: `sequence` rows (submit_sequence, newline_sequence, graceful_exit_sequence) hold the literal PTY bytes a caller writes to the harness master (JSON escapes: \r=CR, \n=LF, \u0003=Ctrl-C, \u001b=ESC); `matcher` rows (composer_signature, permission_prompt_signature) hold the human-readable screen-state description the readiness gate's matcher function implements; `value` rows carry a closed-vocabulary token — honors_bracketed_paste is a boolean, paste_multiline_semantics is one of {`held-multiline-needs-submit`, `auto-submit-on-close`}, mid_generation_semantics is one of {`queued-autosubmit`, `buffered-draft`, `dropped`, `interrupts`}. Rows are derived from tests/probes/session_injection and pinned by that suite's contract tests. Consumed by config.interactionProfile in tui/internal/config/framework.go (the close ladder reads graceful_exit_sequence via HarnessGracefulExitSequence) and by framework_interaction_field in scripts/lib.sh. An absent row or a framework without an interaction block degrades explicitly (`unsupported` / not-supported) with a `[lore] degraded:` notice — callers skip, never substitute a value from another harness.
capabilities.spend_telemetry: Per-framework token-spend exposure (sibling of `capabilities`, `model_routing`, and `interaction`). An evidence-gated block: `support` from the closed level set plus an `evidence` id resolving in capabilities-evidence.md, `artifact` naming the source kind (`transcript` | `rollout` | `store`), `binding` naming the spawn-time join mechanism (`session-id-flag` | `none`), and `fields` listing the normalized token fields the harness's extracted spend object carries (from the closed vocabulary input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, reasoning_output_tokens, total_tokens, cost_usd, model). Extraction runs at the session boundary via adapters/transcripts/<fw>.py::session_spend behind scripts/session-spend.sh (the append-only telemetry it feeds outlives the transcripts, so retrospective bulk mining is out of contract); consumed by config.spendTelemetry in tui/internal/config/framework.go and framework_spend_telemetry_field in scripts/lib.sh. `support: partial` means the artifact carries tokens but no deterministic spawn-time binding is proven, so those sessions degrade to duration-only in v1.

tools.gh_cli: GitHub CLI (`gh`) on PATH for PR/issue read+write workflows. Sourced from the host environment, not the harness — no harness exposes it. Skills that consume PR diffs/comments/reviews require it; degradation is a hard `unsupported` rather than `partial` because the data is unreachable without it.

skills._description: Per-skill capability + tool requirements (T21/T41). Each entry in `requires` is either a string (capability or tool id; treated as `{id, min_level: 'full', partial_below: 'full'}` — i.e., the cell must reach `full` or the skill is `unavailable`) or an object `{id, min_level, partial_below?, notes?}` declaring the threshold below which the skill must refuse and the threshold below which it merely degrades to partial-mode. Resolution rule (consumed by adapter helpers and `lore framework doctor`): for each requirement, look up the active framework's cell support level (or the tool availability for tool ids); if any cell is below `partial_below`, the skill is `unavailable`; if any cell is below `min_level` but at or above `partial_below`, the skill is `partial-mode`; otherwise `full`. The `partial_below` knob is what lets team-heavy skills (bootstrap, implement, spec) run in partial-mode on harnesses where fresh subagents exist but `team_messaging` or `task_completed_hook` are below `full` — without it the skill would have to refuse outright. Skills with an empty `requires` array are framework-agnostic. The set is closed: adding a new skill requires adding a row here; adding a new capability or tool requires adding it to the `capabilities` or `tools` map first.

_degradation_vocab._description: Closed degradation-status vocabulary surfaced by `lore framework doctor` and the orchestration adapter's `[lore] degraded:` stderr notices. T41 mandates these tokens — adapter-emitted strings outside this set are a contract violation.
_degradation_vocab.partial: Skill ran in partial mode: at least one requirement was below its `min_level` but at or above its `partial_below`. Behavior is degraded but functional; the adapter MUST emit a one-line stderr notice naming the specific requirement and its observed level.
_degradation_vocab.fallback: Skill operation routed through a fallback path declared by the requirement (e.g., lead-side validator instead of native blocking hook). Functionality matches `partial` from the user's perspective but the underlying gap is named at the cell level (`fallback` support) rather than the skill level.
_degradation_vocab.none: Skill is `unavailable` because at least one requirement was below `partial_below`. The skill MUST refuse to run; calling it is a no-op + stderr notice naming the missing requirement.
_degradation_vocab.no-evidence: A capability cell the skill depends on has support `> none` but no `evidence` pointer (i.e., the cell exists but has not been verified). Skills SHOULD treat `no-evidence` as a soft block — proceed in partial-mode and surface the missing-evidence notice — until T70 / `lore doctor` is wired to refresh evidence.
_degradation_vocab.unverified-support: Reserved scoped form `unverified-support(<level>)` for stderr notices when a capability cell has been overridden in `settings.json` (per-user override) without a refreshed evidence pointer. Adapter notices use the parenthetical level so the operator sees both the override and the original cell's level.

bootstrap.summary: Bootstrap a knowledge store by exploring codebase architecture with a worker fanout team.

requires.notes: Subagents at `partial`/`fallback` (opencode/codex) downgrade bootstrap to partial-mode (lead-driven exploration with fewer parallel spawns). `none` means refuse — bootstrap cannot work without isolated worker contexts.

team_messaging at any sub-`full` level (including `none` on opencode/codex today) downgrades to lead-orchestrated single-agent exploration (no SendMessage between workers; lead aggregates serially). The skill never refuses on team_messaging alone — see the README skill-behavior matrix; only `task_completed_hook=none` or `subagents=none` triggers refuse.

Hook at `fallback` collapses to `lead_validator` enforcement; bootstrap proceeds with a one-shot stderr degradation notice (per `adapters/agents/README.md` § Skill behavior expectations per mode). `none` means refuse — silent commons corruption otherwise.

bootstrap.notes: Team-heavy skill. partial-mode contract: bootstrap runs with reduced parallelism + lead-side validation when team_messaging or task_completed_hook are below `full`. Hard-refuses on `subagents=none` or `task_completed_hook=none`; `team_messaging=none` is tolerated (lead-only fanout).

evolve.summary: Review and apply accumulated protocol evolution suggestions from retro and self-test.
evolve.notes: Reads/writes knowledge store files inline; no subagent fanout, no PR I/O.

followup-discuss.summary: Structured discussion of followup findings with source-aware routing.
followup-discuss.notes: Interactive conversation; reads knowledge store + work-item state.

implement.summary: Execute a spec's plan with a knowledge-aware worker team and capture observations.

requires.notes: Subagents at `partial`/`fallback` (opencode/codex) downgrade implement to partial-mode — workers still spawn fresh contexts but team-state semantics differ. `none` refuses outright (no spawn surface = no fanout = no implement).

team_messaging below `full` (including `none` on opencode/codex today) removes mid-flight messaging — consultations, shared team state, TeamCreate coordination — not the spawn surface. Step 3.0 of the SKILL.md probes it only when the selection actually requires mid-flight messaging: self-contained tasks stay eligible for worker dispatch via the adapter's spawn → wait → collect_result → shutdown loop, with a one-shot stderr degradation notice; only messaging-requiring selections with no session route collapse to lead-inline. The skill does NOT refuse on team_messaging alone — refuse comes from `subagents=none` or `task_completed_hook=none`.

Hook at `fallback` collapses to `lead_validator` enforcement (D9 fallback). Implement proceeds in partial-mode with the post-hoc validator. `none` refuses — without enforcement the Tier 2 evidence trail and Tier 3 promotion pipeline corrupt silently.

implement.notes: Team-heavy skill. partial-mode contract: implement routes by Step 3.0's operation-level probe — worker dispatch stays available whenever the spawn surface, direct result collection, completion enforcement (`native_blocking`/`lead_validator`), and report materialization hold, with lead-inline reserved for probe-disqualified selections — and still hard-refuses on `subagents=none` or `task_completed_hook=none`. Resolution mirrors `resolve_completion_enforcement_mode` in scripts/lib.sh — when ENFORCEMENT == `unavailable` the skill MUST refuse.

memory.summary: Manage the per-project knowledge store (search, view, curate, heal, init).
memory.notes: Operates on the knowledge store via the lore CLI; no subagent fanout.

pr-blast-radius.summary: Focused lens review: trace PR change impact on code outside the diff.
pr-blast-radius.notes: Reads PR diff via `gh pr diff` and `gh pr view`.

pr-correctness.summary: Focused lens review: trace logic paths for correctness bugs in a PR.

pr-create.summary: Create a GitHub pull request from the current branch.

pr-interface-clarity.summary: Focused lens review: evaluate interface clarity, naming, and abstraction coherence.

pr-pair-review.summary: Interactive pair-review with knowledge-enriched turn protocol.

pr-regressions.summary: Focused lens review: detect capability loss from deletions and modifications.

pr-review.summary: Holistic multi-lens PR review with adaptive lens selection and cross-lens synthesis.
pr-review.notes: Fans out per-lens Agent tasks in parallel; without subagents, falls back to sequential lens execution (slower; same coverage).

pr-revise.summary: Read all PR review feedback and create a work item with categorized findings.

pr-security.summary: Focused lens review: evaluate security vulnerabilities, edge cases, adversarial paths.

pr-self-review.summary: Author-calibrated self-review: parallel lens pre-scan then grounding evaluation.
pr-self-review.notes: Fans out per-lens Skill invocations in parallel; without subagents, falls back to sequential lens execution.

pr-test-quality.summary: Focused lens review: evaluate test coverage, quality, and assertion rigor.

pr-thematic.summary: Focused lens review: evaluate thematic coherence and scope.

pr-user-impact.summary: Focused lens review: evaluate user-facing impact of PR changes.

remember.summary: Capture insights to knowledge store and update conversational threads.
remember.notes: Reads pending-captures queue (set up by stop_hook); the queue surface itself is a stop_hook concern, but `/remember` runs even when the queue is empty so it does not require stop_hook to invoke.

renormalize.summary: Full knowledge store renormalization via orchestrated multi-agent flow.
renormalize.notes: Spawns Explore subagents for parallel category analysis; without subagents, falls back to lead-only sequential renormalization.

retro.summary: Evaluate knowledge system effectiveness after a work cycle.
retro.notes: Reads execution-log.md, worker SendMessage history, and knowledge store; emits a journal entry. No fanout.

self-test.summary: Evaluate the lore memory system across 8 dimensions via subagent evaluators.
self-test.notes: Each dimension runs in a subagent for clean-context evaluation; without subagents, falls back to sequential same-context evaluation (degraded — context contamination affects scores).

spec.summary: Create a technical specification via team-based investigation and synthesis.

requires.notes: Subagents at `partial`/`fallback` downgrade /spec to partial-mode (lead-orchestrated researcher fanout with serial collection). `none` collapses to spec-short single-pass.

/spec is the most permissive of the three team-heavy skills — team_messaging below `full` skips TeamCreate/SendMessage/shared team-state but still permits lead-orchestrated researcher fanout when `subagents` is available. `partial_below: none` means there is no level at which /spec hard-refuses on team_messaging alone; only `subagents=none` collapses to spec-short single-pass.

Hook at `fallback` collapses to `lead_validator`; full /spec proceeds with post-hoc validation per the README skill-behavior matrix. `none` refuses team-mode but spec-short still proceeds (spec-short does not require enforcement because it does not spawn researcher reports).

spec.notes: Team-heavy skill with two degraded shapes: if `subagents` is `partial`/`fallback`, /spec uses lead-orchestrated researcher fanout with serial collection; if `team_messaging` is below `full`, /spec skips TeamCreate/SendMessage and uses a lead-side handle map without suppressing researcher fanout. Only `subagents=none` collapses to spec-short; when `task_completed_hook` drops to `none`, full fanout refuses but spec-short proceeds.

work.summary: Check project status, manage work items, search remaining tasks.
work.notes: Operates via the `lore work` CLI; no harness-side primitives required.

claude-code.description: Reference baseline. Supports the full lore protocol surface: SessionStart/Stop/PreCompact/PreToolUse/SessionEnd/TaskCompleted hooks, ~/.claude/skills + ~/.claude/agents + ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md packaging, TeamCreate/SendMessage team messaging, and JSONL transcripts under ~/.claude/projects/.

model_routing.notes: Single Anthropic provider per session; the role->model map collapses to one binding without affecting call sites.

composer_signature.matcher: a row matching ^\s*[❯>](\s|$) flanked by >=2 full-width rules (^─{80,}$); cursor sits on the prompt row; AND permission_prompt_signature does NOT match (the modal renders in-band inside the composer chrome and its option rows satisfy the structural anchors)
composer_signature.notes: The '❯' prompt row bordered by two pure-'─' full-width rules; persists after the welcome banner scrolls away. Probed in raw/claude-code/p1_composer.log.

permission_prompt_signature.matcher: bottom-region rows with 'Do you want to proceed?' (or footer 'Esc to cancel · Tab to amend · ctrl+e to explain') AND >=2 numbered options ('❯ 1. Yes' / '2. …' / '3. No'); modal header is the tool name ('Bash command'). Scrollback-safe: keyed on the bottom 14 rows.
permission_prompt_signature.notes: A side-effecting command (touch) raises the modal; echo auto-approves off the safe allowlist. Probed in raw/claude-code/p2_permission.log.

submit_sequence.notes: CR (0x0d) submits the composer. Raw LF (0x0a) does NOT submit here — it inserts a newline. Probed in raw/claude-code/p3_submit_newline.log.

newline_sequence.notes: LF (0x0a) inserts a composer newline without submitting (as do alt+enter, shift+enter CSI-u, shift+enter xterm). Probed in raw/claude-code/p3_submit_newline.log.

honors_bracketed_paste.notes: Advertises ESC[?2004h; a 200~..201~-wrapped multiline payload lands as one composer entry. Probed in raw/claude-code/p4_bracketed_paste.log.

paste_multiline_semantics.notes: One multiline composer entry; needs a trailing CR to submit. Probed in raw/claude-code/p4_bracketed_paste.log.

mid_generation_semantics.notes: Type-ahead: bytes injected mid-generation auto-submit as the next turn once the current generation ends — so the readiness gate must be strict (never inject mid-generation). Probed in raw/claude-code/p5_midgen.log.

graceful_exit_sequence.notes: Ctrl-C twice (~0.4s apart) exits; the close ladder writes these bytes to the PTY master as its first rung before SIGTERM. Probed en passant at shared-session teardown.

spend_telemetry.notes: Assistant rows in ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<uuid>.jsonl carry message.usage (input/output/cache_read/cache_creation) and message.model; session_spend sums them and derives total_tokens. Deterministic transcript binding via --session-id at spawn (binding: session-id-flag), so TUI-hosted claude-code sessions close with real token counts.

tui_launch_flags._description: TUI-injected concern -> harness-native CLI flag spelling. Consumed by tui/internal/work/specpanel.go via config.HarnessSystemPromptFlag / HarnessSettingsOverrideFlag and by adapters/agents/<harness>.sh subcommands `system_prompt_flag` / `settings_override_flag`. The literal `unsupported` is the contract token (see install_paths) — callers MUST skip the flag injection rather than substitute a different flag. See adapters/agents/README.md §TUI Launch Concerns for the per-harness rationale.

instructions.notes: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md assembled from claude-md/* fragments by scripts/assemble-claude-md.sh.

session_start_hook.notes: SessionStart hook chain runs auto-reindex -> mine-retrieval-misses -> load-knowledge -> load-work -> load-threads -> extract-session-digest.

stop_hook.notes: Native Stop hook surface is fully supported but lore no longer installs a Stop hook. (check-plan-persistence.py was retired 2026-05-26 — it forced ephemeral builtin plans to be persisted as _work/ items, which produced ghost work items when the model used plan mode for quick scratch planning. stop-novelty-check.py was retired earlier on 2026-05-06 as a low-yield heuristic capture-suggester. See _meta/effectiveness-journal.jsonl and conventions/heuristic-vs-definite-condition-hooks.md.)

pre_compact_hook.notes: PreCompact hook runs scripts/pre-compact.sh; SessionEnd reuses the same script for /clear.

permission_hooks.notes: PreToolUse JSON-stdout `{decision: block|approve}` controls tool invocations.

task_completed_hook.notes: TaskCompleted hook runs task-completed-capture-check.sh; exit 2 + stderr blocks the worker report.

subagents.notes: Fresh subagent contexts via Task / TeamCreate spawn paths.

team_messaging.notes: TeamCreate / SendMessage / shutdown_request with persistent team state under ~/.claude/teams/<name>/.

transcript_provider.notes: JSONL transcripts under ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session-uuid>.jsonl consumed by extract-session-digest. (stop-novelty-check.py and check-plan-persistence.py were consumers until retired 2026-05-06 and 2026-05-26 respectively.)

headless_runner.notes: `claude -p <prompt>` non-interactive mode used by audit-artifact.sh judges.

plugin_runtime.notes: Hooks are shell/python commands invoked by Claude Code; there is no in-process plugin runtime. Scripts handle every extension need.

opencode.description: Multi-provider harness with native Claude-compatible discovery for skills and instruction files. Plugins are JavaScript/TypeScript modules under .opencode/plugins or ~/.config/opencode/plugins. Native team messaging is absent; team workflows degrade to lead-orchestrated fanout/aggregation.

model_routing.notes: Per-spawn provider/model selection; the role->model map honors per-role bindings (e.g., Opus for lead, Haiku for worker fanout). Tiers use the provider/model id shape; the Anthropic ladder is the validated ordered set.

composer_signature.matcher: composer-ready := (row matches /╹▀{5,}/  bottom border) AND (some row contains 'commands' key-hint) AND (left border '┃' present); empty-and-ready adds a row containing 'Ask anything...'; AND permission_prompt_signature does NOT match (the modal renders in-band inside the composer box, leaving the border/hint anchors on screen)
composer_signature.notes: Model-independent glyph anchors: the '╹▀+' bottom border, the 'commands' key-hint, and the '┃' left border; 'Ask anything...' marks the empty-and-ready state. Probed in raw/opencode/p1_composer_launch{1,2}.raw.

permission_prompt_signature.matcher: permission-modal := a row containing '△ Permission required' AND an action row matching /Allow once\s+Allow always\s+Reject/
permission_prompt_signature.notes: Renders in-band in the composer box (no dedicated DECSET layer); requires permission.bash=ask (default config auto-runs bash). Probed in raw/opencode/p2b_permission_modal.raw.

submit_sequence.notes: CR (0x0d) submits the composer. Probed in raw/opencode/p3_submit_newline.raw.

newline_sequence.notes: Alt+Enter (ESC CR, 0x1b 0x0d) inserts a newline without submitting — NOT raw LF, unlike claude-code/codex. Probed in raw/opencode/p3_submit_newline.raw.

honors_bracketed_paste.notes: Advertises ESC[?2004h at composer; 200~..201~ wrap with embedded LF lands as one multiline entry, single trailing CR submits it. Probed in raw/opencode/p4_bracketed_paste.raw.

paste_multiline_semantics.notes: Held as one multiline composer entry; needs a trailing submit. Probed in raw/opencode/p4_bracketed_paste.raw.

mid_generation_semantics.notes: Queued: text injected mid-generation is held (native QUEUED badge) and auto-sent as the next message once generation finishes. Probed in raw/opencode/p5_mid_generation.raw.

graceful_exit_sequence.notes: Single Ctrl-C from a clean idle composer exits (during an active generation ctrl-c/esc interrupts instead — distinguish by screen state); '/exit\r' is the never-ambiguous alternative. The close ladder writes this byte to the PTY master before SIGTERM. Probed in raw/opencode/exit_ctrl_c.raw.

spend_telemetry.notes: The live sqlite store (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db) keys assistant message.data tokens (input/output/reasoning/cache.read/write/total) and a per-message dollar cost by session id; session_spend sums them (basis: store) and degrades to duration-only when only the stale storage/ JSON is reachable. partial because no deterministic session binding exists at spawn, so TUI-hosted opencode sessions stay duration-only in v1.

instructions.notes: OpenCode reads ~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md natively as its global instruction file. Claude-compatible ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md remains a fallback migration path, but lore installs to the OpenCode-native location.

skills.notes: OpenCode discovers SKILL.md from .opencode/skills, ~/.config/opencode/skills, .claude/skills, ~/.claude/skills, .agents/skills, ~/.agents/skills. lore installs to ~/.agents/skills, which OpenCode discovers natively without Claude-compat fallback.

mcp.notes: MCP servers configured in OpenCode config ($HOME/.config/opencode/opencode.json or .opencode/opencode.json); requires per-harness packaging because the path is not ~/.claude/settings.json. Cell is `partial` only because lore's cross-harness writer is not yet wired — the OpenCode surface itself is full.

session_start_hook.notes: Plugin event dispatched at session start; lore-side mapping translates to load-knowledge / load-work / load-threads. Coverage of Lore's pending-capture and pending-digest first-turn pipelines depends on plugin runtime guarantees.

stop_hook.notes: Plugin event for end-of-turn / agent stop; novelty-check fallback may run in degraded mode if the harness does not surface full context for heuristic detection.

pre_compact_hook.notes: Native compaction event semantics not confirmed; fallback path runs the persistence reminder on last-known signal (long session, /clear-equivalent) rather than a true PreCompact event.

tool_hooks.notes: Plugin runtime exposes pre/post tool events; matcher coverage and blocking semantics may differ from PreToolUse.

permission_hooks.notes: Permission decision protocol via plugin events; specific accept/deny shape needs adapter translation.

task_completed_hook.notes: No native subagent-completion blocking event. Lead-side validator checks worker reports after collection and rejects on missing structure (D9 fallback mode: lead-side validator).

subagents.notes: Primary agent + General/Explore subagents for fanout; fresh-context guarantees match Claude's Task spawn but team-state semantics do not.

team_messaging.notes: No native TeamCreate / SendMessage equivalent. Skills requiring inter-agent messaging must run in lead-orchestrated mode (no in-flight worker-to-worker communication).

transcript_provider.notes: Session artifacts exist but format differs from Claude JSONL; provider stub translates available fields and reports degraded for missing ones.

headless_runner.notes: Non-interactive run mode exists; flag spelling differs from `claude -p` so audit-artifact.sh routes through the headless_runner adapter.

plugin_runtime.notes: TypeScript/JavaScript plugin runtime under .opencode/plugins or ~/.config/opencode/plugins; lore ships adapters/opencode/lore-hooks.ts.

codex.description: Codex CLI with native hooks (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PermissionRequest, PostToolUse, Stop) and subagent workflows enabled by default. Native team messaging is absent; PreCompact and TaskCompleted blocking semantics are not covered by the native hook surface and require lead-side fallbacks. The February 2026 'notify-only' premise is stale and superseded by docs/codex-migration.md and the gotchas update in T16.

model_routing.notes: Single provider per session; the role->model map collapses to one binding without affecting call sites. Tiers vary reasoning effort on the shipped gpt-5.5 binding via the adapter's -<effort> suffix split.

composer_signature.matcher: within the bottom 18 rows, a footer suffix matches ·\s+(?:~(?:/|$)|/)\S* and a row at/above it begins with '›' — status badges before the separator are intentionally opaque, and the '›' glyph alone is shared with codex selection menus; AND permission_prompt_signature does NOT match (the negation guards partial repaints)
composer_signature.notes: The bottom-region middot+working-directory suffix is the durable anchor; optional badges such as 'fast' may appear between effort and the separator, and the welcome banner scrolls off after the first turn. Historical probe logs are raw/codex/p1_composer_launch{1,2}.log; the current fast-badge shape is recorded in observations/codex.json.

permission_prompt_signature.matcher: in the bottom 18 rows, an approval or selection action/header anchor plus at least two numbered options and exactly one selected row marked by '›'; the parser exposes the displayed option numbers in row order. A footer clears modal-looking rows as scrollback only when a fresh '›' input row follows the option list before that footer; a footer retained by a partial repaint does not clear the modal
permission_prompt_signature.notes: Only escalates under -a untrusted (default auto-approves trusted commands with no modal). Probed in raw/codex/p2_approval_untrusted.log.

submit_sequence.notes: CR (0x0d) submits the composer. Probed in raw/codex/p3_submit.log.

newline_sequence.notes: Ctrl-J / LF (0x0a) inserts a newline without submitting. Probed in raw/codex/p3_newline_0_ctrl-j-n-0x0a-.log.

honors_bracketed_paste.notes: Composer emits ESC[?2004h at ready; 200~..201~ wrap holds a multiline payload. Probed in raw/codex/p4_bracketed_paste.log.

paste_multiline_semantics.notes: Held as one multiline composer entry; trailing CR submits. Probed in raw/codex/p4_bracketed_paste.log.

mid_generation_semantics.notes: Buffered: text injected mid-turn lands in the composer as an unsent draft (the trailing CR does NOT submit while a turn is active); a fresh CR when idle submits it. Probed in raw/codex/p5_midgen.log.

graceful_exit_sequence.notes: Single Ctrl-C from an idle empty composer exits (with text present the first Ctrl-C clears it, so up to two). The close ladder writes this byte to the PTY master before SIGTERM. Probed in raw/codex/p1_composer_launch{1,2}.log.

spend_telemetry.notes: Rollout token_count events carry cumulative total_token_usage (input/cached_input/output/reasoning/total); session_spend reads the terminal one and normalizes cached_input_tokens -> cache_read_input_tokens (basis: rollout). partial because no deterministic session->rollout binding exists at spawn — rollout paths are date-nested, not addressable by a spawn-time session id — so TUI-hosted codex sessions stay duration-only in v1; the artifact itself parses fully given an explicit --transcript path.

instructions.notes: Codex reads AGENTS.md-style project instructions and layered configuration; assemble-instructions.sh --framework codex emits AGENTS.md instead of CLAUDE.md.

skills.notes: Codex documents a native skills surface; lore packages SKILL.md to the documented Codex skills location. Per-skill discovery semantics may differ from Claude's eager session-start load.

mcp.notes: Codex supports MCP server registration via its config; lore registers servers through the per-harness MCP packaging path.

session_start_hook.notes: Native SessionStart hook documented in current Codex releases; supersedes the stale February assumption that Codex has only `notify`.

stop_hook.notes: Native Stop hook documented in current Codex releases.

pre_compact_hook.notes: No native PreCompact event documented. Fallback uses Stop/SessionStart bookends to remind the user to persist plans before compaction events that lore cannot intercept.

tool_hooks.notes: Native PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks documented in current Codex releases.

permission_hooks.notes: Native PermissionRequest hook documented in current Codex releases.

task_completed_hook.notes: No native subagent-completion blocking event. Lead-side validator checks worker reports after collection (D9 fallback mode: lead-side validator).

subagents.notes: Subagent workflows enabled by default per current docs; Codex orchestrates spawn/follow-up/wait/consolidate when explicitly asked. Fresh-context guarantees match Claude's Task spawn but team-state semantics do not.

team_messaging.notes: No native TeamCreate / SendMessage equivalent. Skills requiring inter-agent messaging must run in lead-orchestrated mode.

transcript_provider.notes: Codex exposes session artifacts; format differs from Claude JSONL so the provider stub translates available fields and reports degraded for missing ones.

headless_runner.notes: Codex supports non-interactive prompt invocation; audit-artifact.sh routes correctness-gate / curator / reverse-auditor through the headless_runner adapter.

plugin_runtime.notes: Codex hooks are shell commands; there is no in-process plugin runtime. Hook scripts handle every extension need.
